title: Body found in river near Brockton water treatment plant, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man's body was discovered floating in a river near Brockton's water treatment plant on Thursday.
Massachusetts State Police retrieved the body, which is unidentified, and sent it to the Chief Medical Examiner.
An investigation is ongoing by Plymouth County DA after similar incidents occurred in 2024 without foul play suspected.

title: Man arrested for alleged assault of rideshare passenger at Logan Airport
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Leonard Bacon, 23, of Lowell, was arrested for allegedly assaulting a rideshare passenger at Logan Airport.
The incident involved a stabbing that occurred Friday morning, leaving the passenger with non-life-threatening injuries.
Bacon is charged with assault and battery with a dangerous weapon and will appear in East Boston District Court.

title: WBZ meteorologist returns after taking 3 years away to care for her son with cancer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sarah Wroblewski returns to WBZ as a meteorologist after a 3-year break to care for her son with cancer.
Her son, Declan, diagnosed with stage 4 medulloblastoma in 2023, is now out of active treatment.
Wroblewski will deliver weather forecasts on weekends and some weekdays with the WBZ Next Weather team.

title: 2 killed in crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth town line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people died in a collision between a Toyota Camry and a Saab on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth.
Tristan Bedient, 34, and Kate Aldrich, 51, were identified as the victims in the Camry, suffering catastrophic damage.
Two Saab occupants sustained non-life-threatening injuries; the crash investigation involves multiple police departments and state police.

title: Bus driver strike ends in Merrimack Valley after nearly 2 weeks
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bus service in Merrimack Valley resumed after a 10-day strike by more than 130 bus drivers and mechanics.
A one-year contract agreement grants a $4 hourly wage increase and preserves Saturday overtime for MeVa workers.
Union and transit officials agreed to continue negotiations, resolving the dispute over Saturday scheduling and compensation.

title: 5 takeaways as red-hot Red Sox rally past Mets, 3-2, for 9th straight victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox win 3-2 against Mets, extending their winning streak to nine games, all on the road.
Boston's pitching staff has been pivotal, allowing two or fewer runs in six consecutive games.
The Red Sox achieved a rare nine-game road sweep, only done once before in franchise history.

title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after a historic four-night concert series at Fenway Park.
Kahan joins Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and the Zac Brown Band in the Hall of Fame, making him the fourth inductee.
The concerts featured fireworks and appearances by Boston sports figures, celebrating his fourth album release 'The Great Divide.'

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from a settlement with Cash App over alleged deceptive safety practices.
The $45 million multistate settlement resolves accusations against Block Inc. for misleading users about Cash App's security.
Cash App allegedly enabled fraud by failing to strengthen security, misleading users, and hosting risky promotions.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old man allegedly took Tyngsborough's police boat and activated its emergency lights.
Police say the man used the boat to enforce local boating rules on Lake Mascuppic.
Authorities plan to charge him with impersonating a police officer after an investigation.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: More than 60 ships from over 20 countries participated in Boston's Sail Boston 2026 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail into Boston Harbor, attracting thousands of visitors.
Tall ships docked at Boston waterfronts concluded their tour with a serenade at Charlestown Navy Yard.
